prompt: Invent an original, vivid metaphor for the experience of learning a new language as an adult. Explain it in three sentences.
response: Learning a new language as an adult is like building a ship in a bottle, already on the open sea. Your hands, clumsy from years of other work, must now perform delicate, precise tasks while the waves of habit and accent constantly rock the hull. The finished vessel, though seaworthy, will always carry the unique craftsmanship of its late construction, sailing beautifully but never quite like one built in the calm of the shipyard of childhood.
